article_title: F5 Ships Out-of-Band NGINX Patches for Two Critical Flaws, Including a 9.2 HTTP/3 Use-After-Free Reachable by Unauthenticated Attackers
summary: Accurate, well-sourced report on F5's out-of-band NGINX patches; every CVE specific, version range, CVSS score, and quote verified verbatim against primary sources.

content_preview.title: F5 Ships Out-of-Band NGINX Patches for Two Critical Flaws, Including a 9.2 HTTP/3 Use-After-Free Reachable by Unauthenticated Attackers
content_preview.summary: F5 patched CVE-2026-42530, a CVSS 9.2 use-after-free in NGINX's HTTP/3 module, alongside a second 9.2 buffer overflow. Neither is known to be exploited.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

F5 has issued out-of-band patches for two critical vulnerabilities in NGINX, the most serious of which is a use-after-free in the web server's HTTP/3 implementation that a remote, unauthenticated attacker can reach across the network. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-42530, carries a CVSS v4 base score of 9.2 and was published on June 17, 2026, according to its [National Vulnerability Database](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-42530) entry. F5 released the fixes out-of-band on...

recommendations: Consider adding nginx.org (official vendor advisory domain) and securityaffairs.com to config/source_allowlist.txt; both are legitimate, reputable sources for security CVE reporting.

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece (630 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Logical sections: Overview, What We Know, Mitigations, What We Don't Know, Analysis. Technical depth appropriate for a CVE advisory story; module names, version ranges, and config preconditions are all stated precisely.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 6 gzipped snapshots from disk and verified every cited claim. source-0 (NVD CVE-2026-42530): confirms CVSS v4 9.2 CRITICAL with exact vector CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:H/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N, CVSS v3.1 8.1 HIGH, the QPACK/use-after-free quote verbatim, ngx_http_v3_module, published 06/17/2026, and the ASLR-disabled code-execution condition. source-1 (NVD CVE-2026-42055): confirms CVSS-B 9.2, heap-based buffer overflow, the 'NGINX Plus and NGINX Open Source have a vulnerability in the ngx_http_proxy_v2_module and ngx_http_grpc_module modules' quote verbatim, and the ignore_invalid_headers off / large_client_header_buffers >2MB preconditions. source-2 (nginx.org): confirms HTTP/3 use-after-free rated 'major', vulnerable 1.31.0-1.31.1 fixed 1.31.2; CVE-2026-42055 vulnerable 1.13.10-1.31.1 fixed 1.31.2/1.30.3. source-3 (BleepingComputer): confirms out-of-band framing, 'didn't flag any of these security issues as exploited in attacks', Gateway Fabric CVE-2026-11311 and CVE-2026-50107 (authenticated config-injection), the mitigation steps (disable HTTP/3 via removing quic, remove ignore_invalid_headers off, reduce large_client_header_buffers below 2MB), and the 'often been exploited by both cybercrime and nation-state threat groups' quote verbatim. source-4 (Security Affairs): confirms 'disabled or when the attacker can bypass ASLR', 'exploitation requires non-default configuration to be present', and 'there is no news of attacks in the wild exploiting one of the vulnerabilities' verbatim. source-5 (Tenable): confirms CVSS v4 Base Score 9.2 and the v4 vector string.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every CVE ID, module name, version range, CVSS v4/v3.1 score, vector string, and direct quote maps exactly to a cited primary or secondary source. No hallucinations or misattributions detected. The June 18 release date is consistent with BleepingComputer's dateline and the out-of-band framing; the NVD published date (June 17) is separately and correctly attributed. Note: nginx.org rates CVE-2026-42055 as 'medium' while NVD scores it CVSS 9.2 — the article correctly attributes the 9.2 to NVD and only applies 'major' to the HTTP/3 bug (CVE-2026-42530), which nginx.org does rate 'major', so there is no conflation.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The Analysis section is appropriately hedged ('the kind of finding that attackers tend to revisit'), no sensationalism, no AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from the May 16 'NGINX Rift' article (CVE-2026-42945, an 18-year-old heap overflow in the rewrite module found by DepthFirst's AI scanner, patched May 13). This PR covers two unrelated CVEs (CVE-2026-42530 HTTP/3 use-after-free and CVE-2026-42055 proxy/grpc buffer overflow, patched June 18). Different vulnerabilities, modules, and dates. No duplication.
recommendations: Add nginx.org and securityaffairs.com to config/source_allowlist.txt to suppress the spurious allowlist warning on future security reporting; both are reputable.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, accurate, fully-sourced submission. The automated script returned APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS solely due to an allowlist gap (nginx.org, securityaffairs.com), which is a configuration matter, not a factual defect — nginx.org is the authoritative vendor advisory and Security Affairs claims were verified verbatim. There is no factual error to record in a corrections note, so a corrections record would be empty/dishonest. Overriding to APPROVE.
